description: Expected output event sequence for Kotoba TTS v1.0 (promptless, one-shot) when synthesizing 'こんにちは、世界。今日もよろしくお願いします。' with response_id='resp_001'

events._comment: Sent by the server in response to the client's open event (after language and speaker_id are validated). `client_id` is the server-assigned session identifier.

Emitted in response to response.create. `response.id` is the id supplied by the client, or a server-generated UUID if it was omitted. The same id appears on every subsequent audio.chunk and on response.done.
Streaming audio deltas. `audio` is base64-encoded pcm_f32 (little-endian float32). `response_id` lets the client correlate chunks back to the originating response.create.
Final audio chunk for this response.
End of this synthesis turn. The session stays open; a new response.create may follow.

cancelled._comment: `usage` reflects tokens consumed before cancellation (partial); omitted entirely if nothing was consumed.

failed_unknown_speaker._comment: No `usage`: this failure occurs before any audio is produced, so nothing was consumed. A failure mid-synthesis carries partial `usage`.

worker_timeout._comment: Non-fatal notification that the server did not produce audio within WS_RESULT_TIMEOUT. The session stays open and synthesis may be retried. After WS_RESULT_TIMEOUT_LIMIT (default 3) consecutive timeouts the server emits a fatal `error` and closes the connection. The `message` field is the literal string the server sends.
